rulings.Case Title: IN RE THE JASMINE LIN IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/5/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed in the related case before an accounting can be approved. The amendment was filed on 5/3/22 (but not actually e-filed until 5/4/22, when it was available for viewing), which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice is defective. In any event, not notice of the First Amendment, etc., appears to have been given. If the conservators are seeking reimbursement out of the trust for fees paid to their attorney without a court order, that is not a personal reimbursement to them. Their attorney is prohibited from receiving payment in advance of an order authorizing the payment. [CRC 7.755(a).] Although this is not the trustee's responsibility, the trustee or its counsel might convey this to them: they are permitted to petition in the conservatorship case for fees to be paid out of the trust. (It is sufficient for the attorney to simply declare that his paralegal fully meets the requirements of Business and Professions Code Section 6450.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

No guardianship case appears to have been opened yet for the three minor children. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTHONY CORBETT RICHMOND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/13/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis and cite probate code sections for the distribution to California Inheritance Group without a court order. 2) If a beneficiary satisfies the requirement of Probate Code 11801, a certified copy of Letters certified within sixty (60) days and issued to the personal representative of the estate of the beneficiary, the original 13100 affidavit executed by the person(s) entitled to the beneficiary's share, or a certified copy of the Spousal/Domestic Partner Property Order issued to the beneficiary's surviving spouse/registered domestic partner must be filed before the hearing date. [LRSF 14.35 G.] 3) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY ALAN HOLLIFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/28/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner must file the original will. (Probate Code 8200 et seq.) 2) Petitioner must file and 15 days give notice on DE-121. 3) Petitioner did not check a required box on page 3 under #5.a. (3) or (4). 4) Petitioner did not check a required box on page 3 under #5.a. (7) or (8) 5) On petition page 2 petitioner did not fill in #3.d. (7). 6) The petition on page 4 #8 does not include the address of Frances Hollifield or date of death. #8 does not include Erin Puruse or Lamona Wood's information. 7) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 8) Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities DE-147. 9) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. 10) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/13/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
